description: Stable RoP-internal identifier for a clinical assessment instrument (cognitive battery, motor exam, neuropsychiatric inventory, functional assessment, quality-of-life scale, composite battery). Format: 'RoP-Instrument:NNNNNNN'. Each version of an instrument is a distinct InstrumentID — MoCA-7.0 and MoCA-8.1 are different rows because their scoring conventions and items differ.

Full instrument name. Examples: 'Montreal Cognitive Assessment', 'Mini-Mental State Examination', 'Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson Disease Rating Scale', 'Neuropsychiatric Inventory', 'Geriatric Depression Scale', 'Functional Activities Questionnaire', 'Clinical Dementia Rating', 'Alzheimer Disease Assessment Scale - Cognitive Subscale'.
Short identifier. Examples: MoCA, MMSE, MDS-UPDRS, NPI, GDS, FAQ, CDR, ADAS-Cog, BBS, 9HPT, TUG, ACE-III, MoCA-B.

Version identifier. Critical because instrument revisions change scoring conventions, items, and total-score interpretation. Examples: 'MoCA-8.1', 'MMSE-2.0', 'MDS-UPDRS-3.0', 'NPI-Q', 'GDS-30', 'GDS-15', 'CDR-2.0', 'ADAS-Cog-13'. Two versions of the same instrument are distinct InstrumentID records.

ISO 639-1 language code of the instrument administration. Different language versions of the same instrument are distinct InstrumentID records because translation and cultural adaptation affect scoring (MoCA-English, MoCA-Japanese, MoCA-Spanish-Mexico are different).

metadata_.format: ISO 639-1 code with optional ISO 3166-1 region (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'es-MX', 'ja', 'zh-CN')

description: Primary functional domain assessed by the instrument. Determines which Tier 11 covariates and which other instruments are appropriate to compare against.

value_definitions.domain-specific-cognition: Single-domain cognitive (e.g., RAVLT for verbal memory, BNT for naming)
value_definitions.functional-IADL: Instrumental activities of daily living (FAQ, Lawton IADL)
value_definitions.composite: Multi-domain composite battery (MDS-UPDRS, ADAS-Cog when used as composite)

description: LOINC panel code when one exists for the full instrument. Many widely-used instruments have LOINC codes (MMSE = 72106-8, MoCA = 72172-0 for total score). Allows direct LOINC-based meta-analysis pooling.

Licensing tier governing use, redistribution, and translation. Some instruments are free; others require per-use fees; others restrict translations to authorized versions only. Affects whether the instrument's items can be reproduced in published RoP collections.

DOI of the primary validation publication. Critical for reproducibility — analyses citing instrument scores should cite the validation paper, not just the instrument name. Examples: MoCA → Nasreddine et al 2005 (10.1111/j.1532-5415.2005.53221.x), MDS-UPDRS → Goetz et al 2008 (10.1002/mds.22340).

Plausibility bounds for the instrument's total score. JSON object {min, max, valid_increments}. Used for outlier detection and unit-error catching: a MoCA score of 38 is impossible (max=30), a GDS-15 score of 25 is impossible (max=15). Not clinical reference ranges (those are policy/locality-specific) — just plausibility for catching data errors.

schema.valid_increments: scoring increment (1.0 for integer scores, 0.5 for half-point scoring)

description: Direction of severity for the total score. Critical for downstream interpretation: cognitive batteries are typically higher-is-better (more correct items) while depression/severity scales are typically higher-is-worse. Confusing the direction is the most common scoring error in cross-cohort meta-analyses.

value_definitions.higher-is-better: Higher score indicates better function (MoCA, MMSE, ADAS-Cog (note: ADAS is reverse — higher=worse, common confusion))
value_definitions.higher-is-worse: Higher score indicates more severe pathology (GDS, NPI, MDS-UPDRS, ADAS-Cog)
value_definitions.both-extremes-are-worse: U-shaped scoring where extreme values in either direction indicate dysfunction

metadata_.rationale: Sign errors in score direction are silent: a meta-analysis combining MoCA with MMSE (both higher-is-better) is fine, but combining MoCA with ADAS-Cog (opposite direction) without sign correction produces meaningless results.

description: Sub-scale decomposition for instruments that yield component scores. JSON array of {subscale_name, score_range, item_numbers}. Examples: MDS-UPDRS has Parts I/II/III/IV; MoCA has visuospatial/naming/attention/language/abstraction/delayed-recall/orientation; ADAS-Cog has cognitive subdomain breakdown. Allows component-score CDEs to be defined and validated.

schema_per_entry.subscale_name: Name of the sub-scale (e.g., 'Part III - Motor Examination')
schema_per_entry.item_numbers: JSON array of item numbers contributing to the sub-scale
schema_per_entry.loinc_code: LOINC code for the sub-score when one exists

description: Reference to normative data when Z-score or standardized score conversion is supported. JSON object describing the normative population (cohort name, age range, education range, source publication). Permits cross-cohort comparison via standardized scores even when raw scores aren't directly comparable.

schema.z_score_formula: formula for converting raw to Z (typically '(raw - mean) / SD')
